products.name: Segway Ninebot KickScooter Max G2
products.verdict: The best electric scooter overall — 43-mile range, Apple Find My, and built-in turn signals in a tank-like build.
products.description: The Segway Ninebot Max G2 is the electric scooter that does everything well. Its 43-mile max range is the longest in the commuter class, powered by a 551Wh battery and a 450W motor that hits 22 mph. Built-in turn signals, a front suspension fork, and Apple Find My tracking add safety and peace of mind. The 10" pneumatic tires absorb bumps, and the IPX5 water resistance handles rain. It's the scooter that turns a commute into a joy ride.
pros: Built-in turn signals are a huge safety upgrade
Apple Find My for theft tracking
cons: Heavy at 44 lbs — hard to carry upstairs
22 mph feels slow for experienced riders

products.name: Xiaomi Electric Scooter 4 Pro
products.verdict: The best value premium scooter — 30-mile range, dual brakes, and a sleek design at a competitive price.
products.description: The Xiaomi Electric Scooter 4 Pro delivers premium performance at a mid-range price. Its 700W peak motor pushes to 20 mph, and the 446Wh battery provides up to 30 miles of range. The 10" tubeless pneumatic tires are puncture-resistant, and dual braking (regenerative + disc) stops you quickly and safely. The minimalist design with an LED dashboard, app connectivity, and cruise control makes it the most refined scooter in its price range.
cons: No suspension — relies on tire absorption

products.name: GOTRAX G4
products.verdict: The best budget commuter scooter — 25-mile range and 20 mph speed for under $500.
products.description: The GOTRAX G4 packs impressive specs into a budget-friendly package. Its 350W motor reaches 20 mph, and the 375Wh battery delivers up to 25 miles of range — more than enough for most daily commutes. 10" pneumatic tires provide a comfortable ride, the dual braking system (electronic + rear disc) stops safely, and the foldable design makes it easy to carry on public transit. At under $500, it's the smartest entry into electric scooter commuting.
pros: Foldable for public transit and storage
cons: Build quality is adequate, not premium

products.name: Apollo City Pro (2023)
products.verdict: The best performance commuter — dual suspension, 28 mph, and a 38-mile range in a foldable package.
products.description: The Apollo City Pro bridges the gap between commuter scooters and performance machines. Its 500W sustained (1000W peak) motor pushes to 28 mph, and the 748Wh battery provides up to 38 miles of range. Dual spring suspension front and rear absorbs city potholes, and the triple braking system (regenerative + dual disc) provides confident stopping power. IP66 water resistance handles any weather, and the foldable design makes it practical for mixed commutes.
pros: Dual suspension smooths out rough roads
cons: Heavy at 53 lbs — awkward to carry
Requires more maintenance than budget scooters

products.name: Unagi Model One Voyager
products.verdict: The most beautiful electric scooter — dual motors, carbon fiber stem, and a design that turns heads.
products.description: The Unagi Model One Voyager is the scooter for people who care about aesthetics as much as performance. Its magnesium alloy deck, carbon fiber handlebar stem, and Japanese-designed silhouette make it the best-looking scooter on the market. Dual 250W motors (front + rear) provide all-wheel traction and hill-climbing ability, and the 500Wh battery delivers up to 25 miles of range. At 26 lbs, it's one of the lightest dual-motor scooters available.
pros: 26 lbs is light for a dual-motor scooter
Dual motors provide great hill performance
cons: Solid tires — harsher ride on rough roads
Electronic brake stopping distance is longer

products.name: Hiboy S2 Pro
products.verdict: The best budget scooter with solid tires — maintenance-free 10" solids and 25-mile range at a low price.
products.description: The Hiboy S2 Pro is perfect for riders who never want to deal with flat tires. Its 10" solid honeycomb tires are puncture-proof and maintenance-free, while the 350W motor delivers 19 mph and the 36V battery provides up to 25 miles of range. The rear dual suspension absorbs bumps that solid tires would otherwise transmit, and the foldable design with app connectivity makes it a practical daily commuter. At $459, it's outstanding value for a solid-tire scooter.
pros: Rear suspension compensates for solid tires
25-mile range at this price is competitive
Foldable and relatively light at 36 lbs
cons: Solid tires transmit more vibration than pneumatic

products.name: Segway Ninebot F2 Pro
products.verdict: The best mid-range Segway — 33-mile range, front suspension, and Segway reliability at a reasonable price.
products.description: The Segway Ninebot F2 Pro slots between budget scooters and the flagship Max G2, hitting a sweet spot of price and performance. Its 400W motor reaches 20 mph, and the 460Wh battery provides an impressive 33 miles of range. The front suspension fork smooths out bumps, 10" pneumatic tires grip well, and the Segway app provides ride stats, speed mode settings, and firmware updates. At $649, it's the best Segway for riders who want great range without the Max G2 price tag.

products.name: GOTRAX XR Ultra
products.verdict: The best ultra-budget scooter — a foldable commuter with 17-mile range for under $350.
products.description: The GOTRAX XR Ultra is the cheapest electric scooter worth buying for daily use. Its 300W motor reaches 15.5 mph, and the 187Wh battery provides up to 17 miles of range — enough for short commutes and errands. The 8.5" pneumatic tires provide a smoother ride than solid alternatives, and the foldable design makes it easy to store under a desk or carry onto a bus. At $349, it's the gateway into electric scooter commuting.
pros: Pneumatic tires ride smoother than solids
cons: 15.5 mph is slow for road use

products.name: NIU KQi3 Max
products.verdict: The best-riding mid-range scooter — front + rear suspension, 40-mile range, and a refined riding experience.
products.description: The NIU KQi3 Max comes from NIU, the world's largest electric two-wheeler company, and the engineering shows. Dual front and rear suspension provide the smoothest ride in the mid-range category, and the 450W motor with 40-mile range handles extended commutes without range anxiety. 9.5" tubeless pneumatic tires resist punctures, the regenerative braking + drum brake system stops confidently, and the NIU app provides ride tracking and firmware updates.
pros: Dual suspension provides the smoothest ride
40-mile range is exceptional for the price
NIU's two-wheeler expertise shows in ride quality

products.name: Razor E Prime III
products.verdict: The best lightweight commuter — just 22 lbs with a foldable design for easy last-mile transit connections.
products.description: The Razor E Prime III is built for the last-mile problem — getting from the bus stop or train station to your office. At just 22 lbs and foldable, it's the lightest adult electric scooter that's actually usable. The 250W hub motor reaches 18 mph, and the tubeless pneumatic front tire provides some cushion. The anti-rattle folding mechanism stays tight over time, and the rear fender brake provides simple, reliable stopping. It's not the fastest or longest-range, but it's the easiest to carry.
pros: 22 lbs is lightest in class — easy to carry
Foldable design is compact for transit
18 mph is decent for last-mile use
cons: Fender brake is less effective than disc
